examples.expected_logan_ql: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and (('Process Name' like '*\\powershell.exe') and ('Command Line' like '* -enc *' or 'Command Line' like '* -EncodedCommand *'))
examples.explanation: Sigma logsource and field modifiers map through config/sigma_oci_mapping.yaml into OCI display fields.
examples.source_query:
title: PowerShell Encoded Command
id: demo-sigma-ps-001
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -EncodedCommand '
  condition: selection
level: high
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Windows Sysmon Events with Event ID 1, Process Name, Command Line, User Name.
examples.title: Sigma PowerShell Encoded Command

examples.expected_logan_ql: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Security Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Event Security Logs') and ('Event ID' = '4688') and ('Command Line' like '* -enc *') | stats count as Hits by Entity, 'Subject User Name' | sort -Hits
examples.explanation: The Sentinel table maps to Windows security log sources, has maps to like, and summarize maps to stats.
examples.source_query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4688
| where CommandLine has " -enc "
| summarize Hits=count() by Computer, SubjectUserName
| order by Hits desc
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Windows Security Events with Event ID 4688 and Command Line.
examples.title: Sentinel KQL SecurityEvent PowerShell

examples.expected_logan_ql: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Application Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Event System Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Security Events' or 'Log Source' = 'OCI Cloud Guard Problems') and (('Original Log Content' like '*Contoso*' or msg like '*Contoso*')) | head 10
examples.explanation: KQL search table scopes resolve to mapped OCI sources, and free-text search terms use common raw-message fields.
examples.synthetic_log_shape: SOC Application Logs plus Windows Event and alert-shaped logs with raw message text.
examples.title: KQL Search Across Perf Event Alert

examples.expected_logan_ql: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Security Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Event Security Logs') and ('Event ID' in ('4624', '4625')) | eval Outcome = if('Event ID' = '4625', 'failed', if('Event ID' = '4624', 'success', 'other')) | stats count as Count by Entity, Outcome | sort -Count | head 20
examples.explanation: Common KQL operators map to Logan filters and pipeline commands: explicit time windows are delegated to the dashboard range, case becomes eval/if, summarize-by becomes a counted distinct rollup, and take becomes head.
examples.source_query:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(1d) .. now())
| where EventID in (4624, 4625)
| extend Outcome = case(EventID == 4625, 'failed', EventID == 4624, 'success', 'other')
| summarize by Computer, Outcome
| sort by Count
| take 20
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Windows Security Events with Event ID, host entity, and outcome classification.
examples.title: KQL Common Operators SecurityEvent Outcomes

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' and 'Event ID' = '1' and 'Process Name' like '*\\powershell.exe' and 'Command Line' like '* -enc *' | stats count as count by 'Host Name', 'User Name', 'Command Line' | sort -count | head 100
examples.explanation: SPL source constraints become the Logan base search; stats/sort are retained as pipe commands.
examples.source_query: index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Sysmon EventCode=1 Image="*\\powershell.exe" CommandLine="* -enc *" | stats count by host User CommandLine | sort -count
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Windows Sysmon Events with host, user, process, and command line fields.
examples.title: Splunk SPL Encoded PowerShell
warnings: SPL conversion is heuristic unless a backend parser plugin is configured.

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' and 'Event ID' = '1' and 'Process Name' like '*powershell.exe' and ('Command Line' like '*enc*' or 'Command Line' like '*EncodedCommand*') | head 100
examples.explanation: ECS process fields map to OCI process display fields and wildcard terms map to like predicates.
examples.source_query: event.code:1 AND process.name:powershell.exe AND process.command_line:(*enc* OR *EncodedCommand*)
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Windows Sysmon Events with ECS-like process fields mapped by parser.
examples.title: Elastic Lucene PowerShell Process
warnings: Analyzer and nested-document behavior are not represented in Logan QL.

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'SOC Application Logs' and 'Response Code' = 403 and 'Request Method' = 'POST' | stats count as hits by 'Service Name', 'Trace ID', 'Source IP' | sort -hits | head 100
examples.explanation: Self-authored Kuery example mapping ECS HTTP fields to SOC Application Logs display fields.
examples.synthetic_log_shape: SOC Application Logs with Response Code, Request Method, Service Name, Trace ID, and Source IP.
examples.title: Elastic Kuery HTTP POST 403
warnings: Analyzer-specific KQL behavior is not represented in Logan QL.

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'SOC Application Logs' and 'Response Code' >= 500 and 'Service Name' is not null | stats count as errors by 'Service Name' | sort -errors | head 20
examples.explanation: Self-authored ES|QL pipeline example mapping FROM/WHERE/STATS/SORT/LIMIT into Logan QL.
examples.source_query:
FROM logs-apm*
| WHERE http.response.status_code >= 500 and service.name is not null
| STATS errors = count(*) BY service.name
| SORT errors DESC
| LIMIT 20
examples.synthetic_log_shape: SOC Application Logs with Response Code and Service Name.
examples.title: Elastic ES|QL HTTP Error Rollup
warnings: Only common ES|QL pipeline commands are converted in the first implementation tranche.

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'OCI Audit Logs' and 'Original Log Content' like '*authentication*' and Status = 'failure' | stats count as event_count by 'Source IP', 'User Name' | where event_count >= 5 | head 100
examples.explanation: Self-authored Elastic-style TOML threshold example dispatching metadata to the Kuery converter and threshold aggregation path.
examples.source_query:
[rule]
type = "threshold"
language = "kuery"
name = "Synthetic failed authentication threshold"
query = '''event.category:authentication and event.outcome:failure'''

[rule.threshold]
field = ["source.ip", "user.name"]
value = 5
examples.synthetic_log_shape: OCI Audit Logs with Source IP, User Name, Event Category, and Status fields.
examples.title: Elastic TOML Synthetic Threshold
warnings: Threshold windows and schedules must be validated against the OCI saved-search schedule.

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'SOC Application Logs' and (('Request URL' like '*<script*' or 'Request URL' like '*javascript:*'))
examples.explanation: The app/APM parser exposes Request URL and Trace ID on SOC Application Logs.
examples.source_query:
title: Reflected XSS In Browser Telemetry
id: demo-sigma-xss-001
logsource:
  product: web
detection:
  selection:
    url|contains:
      - '<script'
      - 'javascript:'
  condition: selection
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
examples.synthetic_log_shape: SOC Application Logs OpenTelemetry-shaped JSON with Request URL and Trace ID.
examples.title: Sigma SOC Application XSS

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'Azure Entra ID Audit Logs' and (Operation = 'UserLoggedIn') and (Status = 'success') | stats count as Attempts by Operation, Status | sort -Attempts
examples.explanation: Cloud audit table predicates map to audit source fields and aggregate by supported operation/status fields.
examples.source_query:
AuditLogs
| where OperationName == "UserLoggedIn"
| where Result == "success"
| summarize Attempts=count() by OperationName, Result
| order by Attempts desc
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Cloud audit log with operation, status, target resource, and principal.
examples.title: Sentinel Audit Sign-In Without MFA

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'SOC Application Logs' and 'Request URL' like '*search*' and ('Request URL' like '*<script*' or 'Request URL' like '*onerror*') and 'Response Code' = 200 | stats count as hits by 'Service Name', 'Trace ID', 'Source IP' | sort -hits
examples.explanation: HTTP URL and response fields map to SOC Application Logs display fields.
examples.source_query: url.path:*search* AND url.query:(*<script* OR *onerror*) AND http.response.status_code:200
examples.synthetic_log_shape: SOC Application Logs with Request URL, Response Code, Source IP.
examples.title: Elastic Web XSS
warnings: Escaping behavior depends on the parser-normalized request URL.

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'OCI VCN Flow Logs' | lookup threat_ips 'Source IP' | where threat is not null | stats count as count by 'Source IP', threat
examples.explanation: Lookups require a pre-created OCI lookup table; the frontend must surface that dependency.
examples.source_query: index=network src_ip=* | lookup threat_ips ip as src_ip OUTPUT threat | where isnotnull(threat) | stats count by src_ip threat
examples.synthetic_log_shape: VCN flow logs with Source IP plus an OCI lookup table named threat_ips.
examples.title: Splunk Threat IP Lookup
warnings: lossy_lookup: requires OCI lookup table threat_ips

examples.explanation: True cross-table joins are blocked until a backend correlation strategy exists.
examples.source_query:
SecurityEvent
| join kind=inner (SigninLogs) on Account
| summarize count() by Account
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Requires multiple source tables; not represented by one Logan query.
examples.title: Sentinel Unsupported Cross-Table Join

examples.explanation: OSQuery SQL runs on endpoint state, so Logan Forge only converts OSQuery result logs, not raw endpoint SQL execution.
examples.source_query: select name, cmdline from processes where name = 'powershell.exe';
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Requires an OSQuery result log source before Logan QL can search results.
examples.title: OSQuery SQL Endpoint State Boundary

examples.explanation: YARA scans file content; Logan Forge converts YARA match result logs only when a real OCI log source emits those events.
examples.synthetic_log_shape: Requires a YARA match result log source before Logan QL can search matches.
examples.title: YARA Content Scan Boundary

examples.expected_logan_ql: 'Log Source' = 'SOC Application Logs' and 'Response Code' >= 500 | stats count as errors by 'Service Name' | sort -errors | head 20
examples.explanation: OCI Logan QL inputs pass through with validation and contextual docs.
examples.source_query: 'Log Source' = 'SOC Application Logs' and 'Response Code' >= 500 | stats count as errors by 'Service Name' | sort -errors | head 20
examples.synthetic_log_shape: SOC Application Logs with Response Code and Service Name.
examples.title: OCI Logan QL Passthrough
